modules.name: m_lib.metaclasses
documentation: To solve "TypeError: metatype conflict among bases"

functions.name: m_lib.metaclasses.child
documentation:
Class factory avoiding metatype conflicts: if the base classes have
metaclasses conflicting within themselves or with the given metaclass,
it automatically generates a compatible metaclass and instantiate the
child class from it. The recognized keywords in the option dictionary
are name, dic and meta.

classes.name: m_lib.pbar.tty_pbar.ttyProgressBar

methods.name: m_lib.pbar.tty_pbar.ttyProgressBar.display
documentation:
Draw current value on indicator.
Optimized to draw as little as possible.

Simple progress indicator - displays bar "graph" using standard tty
commands - space, backspace, etc. This method is compatible with
(almost) all UNIX consoles and DOS boxes.

Example:

   ====------  42%

This is a "bar" (width 10 chars) for 42%

Certainly, to use it nicely, do not write anything on screen
(to stdout or stderr), while using it (or use erase()/redraw() methods).
Erase or delete it after using.

functions.name: m_lib.opdate.IncDate
documentation:
Add (or subtract) the number of months, days, and years to a date.
Months and years are added before days. No overflow/underflow checks are made

functions.name: m_lib.tty_menu.hmenu
documentation:
Writes prompt and read result
until result[0] is one of allowed characters (from astring),
and returns the character

functions.name: m_lib.tty_menu.vmenu
documentation:
Prints numbered list of items and allow user to select one,

modules.name: m_lib.net.ftp.ftpparse
m_lib.hash
documentation:
Extended disk hashes package.

It extends anydbm/whichdb with ZODB and MetaKit-based hashes.

functions.name: m_lib.net.ftp.ftpscan.ftpscan1
documentation:
Recursive FTP scan using one-by-one directory traversing. It is slow
but robust - it works with all but very broken FTP servers.

functions.name: m_lib.net.ftp.ftpscan.ftpscanrecursive
documentation:
Recursive FTP scan using fast LIST -R command. Not all servers supports
this, though.

documentation: CompressedShelf implementation that also compresses keys.

modules.name: m_lib.flad
documentation: Broytman Flat ASCII Database for Python

modules.name: m_lib.flad.flad
documentation:
Flat ASCII Database.
This module implements a very simple database on the flat ASCII files.

functions.name: m_lib.flad.flad.load_file
documentation: Create a database object and load it from file

functions.name: m_lib.flad.flad.load_from_file
documentation: Create a database object and load it from file

classes.name: m_lib.flad.flad.Flad

methods.name: m_lib.flad.flad.Flad.__init__
documentation: Field and record separators are ALWAYS newline. It cannot be changed
without extensive rewriting of this module

methods.name: m_lib.flad.flad.Flad.split_key
documentation: Split input line to key/value pair and add the pair to dictionary

methods.name: m_lib.flad.flad.Flad.load_file
documentation:
Load a database from file as a list of records.
Every record is a dictionary of key/value pairs.
The file is reading as whole - this is much faster, but require
more memory.

methods.name: m_lib.flad.flad.Flad.load_from_file
documentation:
Load a database from file as a list of records.
Every record is a dictionary of key/value pairs.
The file is reading line by line - this is much slower, but do not
require so much memory. (On systems with limited virtual memory,
like DOS, it is even faster - for big files)

Class to represent memory database.
FLAD database is a list of records,
where every record is a dictionary.

functions.name: m_lib.flad.fladm.check_record
documentation: Check record for consistency and append it to list of records

functions.name: m_lib.flad.fladm.load_file
documentation: Create a database object and load it from file

functions.name: m_lib.flad.fladm.load_from_file
documentation: Create a database object and load it from file

classes.name: m_lib.flad.fladm.Flad_WithMustKeys
documentation:
Database with two lists of keys - keys that must be in every record,
and keys that allowed to be in some records.

modules.name: m_lib.flad.fladw
documentation: Flat ASCII Database to load WIN.INI-like files.

classes.name: m_lib.flad.fladw.Flad_WIni
documentation:
FLAD database is a list of records, where every record is
a tuple (section_name, keys, section_dictionary).
Sounds similary to Flad? But it is Flad! The only difference is that
Flad_WIni has section names and keys (i.e. list of keys and comments
in every section to preserve comments and desired order of keys).

modules.name: m_lib.m_shutil
documentation: Shell utilities. Additional to shutil.py (standard library module).

functions.name: m_lib.mcrypt.gen_salt
documentation:
There are some difference among modern unicies. BSD/OS, for example,
uses MD5 hash, and ignores salt completely. FreeBSD uses 3 different
versions of crypt() - with standard salt, with extended 9-byte salt,
and MD5 (again, ignoring salt at all).
This function generates salt for standard "Broken DES"-based crypt().

modules.name: m_lib.md5wrapper
documentation: User wrapper for md5 builtin object
